positional_bias.notes: 6 MCQs; 0-based correct positions: q1=1, q2=3, q3=0, q4=1, q5=2, q6=3. Distribution pos0=1, pos1=2, pos2=1, pos3=2. max_share=0.333 <= 0.40. Matches quiz.meta.json correct_position_distribution (pos1–pos4 1-based keys map to 0-based pos0–pos3 correctly). No reshuffle needed.

length_bias.notes: 2 of 6 questions (0.333) show longest-correct pattern; hard-fail threshold is >0.40 (>2 of 6 questions). q5 correct answer is 41% longer than distractor mean; q6 correct is 57% longer than distractor mean. Both exceed the 20% flag threshold but the rate stays at 0.333, under the 0.40 hard-fail line. Flagged for builder awareness on next revision.

distractor_plausibility.notes: 18 total distractors across 6 questions. No distractor scored 1 (absurd). Two distractors scored 2 (wrong but not anchored in a common misconception): q5 'System 2 stores System 1's impression in long-term memory for future pattern recognition' and q6 'AI-generated text is formatted to bypass System 2 entirely'. Both are weak but not absurd and do not exceed the 0.25 absurd_rate hard-fail threshold. All other distractors scored 3–4 as plausible beginner misconceptions about System 2.

covered_objectives: LO1 — Explain what System 2 thinking is and describe its three defining characteristics
LO2 — Distinguish System 2 from System 1 using concrete examples
LO3 — Describe what working memory is and explain why it limits System 2
LO4 — Identify conditions that cause System 2 to activate
LO5 — Explain what cognitive fatigue is and describe how it affects System 2
LO6 — Describe System 2's role in checking and overriding System 1
LO7 — Apply awareness of System 2 limitations to explain why humans don't always catch AI errors
objective_coverage.notes: All 7 LOs covered: q1→LO1, q2→LO2, q3→LO3, q4→LO4+LO5 (combined), q5→LO6, q6→LO7. No orphan questions. LO4 and LO5 share q4 which is permissible given the 6-question standard-profile count. Sidecar objective_ref values match corpus §3 LO text with high similarity.

beginner_scope.notes: All stems use plain language with no unexplained acronyms. No forward references to 9.3+ concepts, confirmation bias, anchoring, automation bias, or the Judgment Framework. AI-oversight framing in q4 and q6 is grounded in corpus §6 (worked example) and §8 (Connection to AI Context), both in-scope for topic 9.2. Distractors draw only on within-topic concepts or plausible beginner confusions about dual-process theory. No out_of_scope_question findings.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean; positional bias 0.333, length bias 0.333 (two questions flagged but under hard-fail threshold), zero absurd distractors, all 7 LOs covered, no out-of-scope content.
